The PCAOB announced the appointment of Dr. Ying Li Compton as its Chief Economist and Director of the Office of Economic and Risk Analysis.
Chief Auditor Barbara Vanich will leave the PCAOB after more than 17 years of service.
The PCAOB today announced the establishment of a Firm Consultation Process designed to provide timely and consistent guidance from PCAOB staff.
The PCAOB has launched an open comment period for stakeholders to provide input on its standard-setting and research agendas.
The PCAOB announced the appointment of George G. Demos as Director of its Division of Enforcement and Investigations.
The PCAOB has published its annual report on broker-dealer audits.
The PCAOB today issued for public comment a proposal regarding narrow, targeted amendments to QC 1000, A Firm’s System of Quality Control.
The PCAOB will hold an open meeting to consider issuing proposed amendments related to QC 1000, the PCAOB’s quality control standard, for public comment.

The PCAOB is seeking investors, audit committee members, auditors, financial executives, and other experts to serve on a new advisory body: the Inspections Modernization Council. Applications are due on June 15, 2026.
Randy L. Thornton has been named the PCAOB’s Chief Operating Officer.
The PCAOB has posted updates to its standard-setting, research, and rulemaking projects.

The PCAOB Office of the Investor Advocate is alerting investors to the opportunity to provide the PCAOB Investor Advisory Group with examples of critical audit matters or key audit matters for analysis.
The PCAOB will hold a meeting of its Standards and Emerging Issues Advisory Group on May 6, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. ET.
In 2025, the PCAOB staff gathered insights from more than 250 audit committee chairs. A new staff Spotlight presents high-level observations and takeaways from this engagement.
The PCAOB will hold a meeting of its Investor Advisory Group on April 29, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. ET.
The PCAOB announced the appointment of George G. Demos as Director of its Division of Enforcement and Investigations.
The PCAOB has sanctioned an audit firm, an owner of that firm, and a former audit manager for multiple violations of PCAOB rules and standards.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a CPA for violations related to audit evidence and her former audit firm for quality control issues.
The PCAOB has sanctioned an audit firm for repeatedly failing to make required disclosures about who worked on audits.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a U.S. audit firm for violations that include failure to reasonably supervise an unregistered China-based public accounting firm.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a former audit partner for multiple violations of PCAOB rules and standards.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a U.S. audit firm for violations related to communications between predecessor and successor auditors.

The PCAOB has permanently revoked the registration of Hong Kong firm Centurion ZD CPA & Co. and permanently barred the firm’s owner for numerous violations of PCAOB rules and standards, including in connection with Chinese company audits.
The PCAOB has sanctioned PWR CPA LLP for failing to conduct inquiries regarding fraud risks and other repeated violations.
The PCAOB has sanctioned two firms for violations related to required audit records and disclosure of key information for investors.
The PCAOB has sanctioned Michael Coglianese, CPA P.C., for engagement quality review failures on 17 engagements.
PCAOB imposes a total of $8.5 million in fines on three Dutch firms for quality control violations related to widespread training exam misconduct.
The PCAOB has permanently barred Michael T. Studer, CPA, and permanently revoked the PCAOB registration of his firm, Michael T. Studer CPA P.C.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a firm and one of its partners for, among other violations, failing to properly document five audits.
The PCAOB has sanctioned Adeptus Partners LLC and a partner for violations related to supervision, review, and quality control.
The PCAOB has sanctioned James Pai CPA PLLC and its sole partner for audit failures.
The PCAOB has sanctioned an engagement partner for multiple audit failures and a violation of independence requirements.
The PCAOB has sanctioned PwC Singapore for violations of PCAOB rules and quality control standards.
The PCAOB announced settled disciplinary orders sanctioning nine firms from KPMG’s global network for violations of PCAOB rules and standards, including quality control standards.
The PCAOB has sanctioned a former engagement partner for violating multiple PCAOB rules and standards, including failure to cooperate with a PCAOB inspection.
